en: Oh, that's like asking me to choose between my children, literally.
ko: 아이들 중에 고르라는 것과 같아요. 말 그대로 이 프로그램들을 하는 동안 아이들이 태어났거든요

en: So my job as a composer starts at something called a spotting session, which is a meeting in which the composer meets with the producers, the directors, the writers, music supervisors and other members of the crew to watch an episode all the way through and talk about where music should go in any given scene, whether there should be music or shouldn't be music and whether that music should be composed or licensed and hopefully some insight into what it should sound like.

en: I don't think I'm ever confident enough to say that I nailed anything.
It's always a tricky thing scoring for TV and film, but I can share an interesting story that relates to Better Call Saul.
ko: TV와 영화 음악은 항상 까다롭지만 베터 콜 사울과 관련된

en: We were working on the last season and I wrote, I was working on one of the later episodes that begins with Howard Hamlin's car left at the beach.

It's one of the last things I shot for the show and I wrote, I was working through that episode sequentially and I wrote a piece of music for the beginning of that and then as I, and I liked it and I was moving through it, there's a very famous scene at the end where they bury Howard Hamlin and I realized that the music I wrote for early in the episode would really actually work much better there and so we ended up moving it there and I just instinctively knew that that I had found the right idea only I had found it in the wrong spot.
